extractions.problem: Sample heterogeneity in microarray data from mixed cell populations confounds identification of differentially expressed genes and gene expression-based classification.
extractions.method: Linear model-based computational framework using optimization to invert sample heterogeneity, estimating pure cell expression values and mixing percentages, with PCA for global performance evaluation.
extractions.finding: The method successfully reconstructs both sample and cell type-specific expression values from heterogeneous mixtures and estimates mixing percentages; a general-purpose model selection method determines the correct number of cell types.
extractions.dataset: cDNA microarray of 5 heterogeneous mixtures of lymph node and RKO colon cancer cells, 4704 human genes
extractions.result: Successfully reconstructed pure cell profiles from 5 mixture ratios with known mixing percentages

extractions.problem: Understanding how DMSO enhances cell membrane permeability in cholesterol-containing membranes and whether MD-predicted pore formation regimes translate to living cells.
extractions.method: Molecular dynamics simulations of DOPC/20% cholesterol bilayers with varying DMSO concentrations, validated by DC-3F cell experiments measuring permeability to water, Ca2+ and Yo-Pro-1.
extractions.finding: Three DMSO concentration-dependent regimes (membrane loosening, stable pore formation, bilayer collapse) are reproduced in cholesterol-containing membranes and confirmed in living cells with size-dependent molecular permeability.
extractions.dataset: DOPC/20% cholesterol bilayer MD simulations, DC-3F cell cultures
extractions.metric: Membrane structural properties (area per lipid, order parameters), cell permeability to water/Ca2+/Yo-Pro-1
extractions.result: Three regimes confirmed: ≤10 mol% transient pores, ~15 mol% stable pores, ≥40 mol% bilayer collapse

extractions.problem: Understanding how metabolic allometric scaling emerges in cell aggregates when accounting for intrinsic fluctuations in mass and metabolic rate, which are typically neglected.
extractions.method: In silico pipeline using finite element simulations of reaction-diffusion (Fick's law + Michaelis-Menten kinetics) in randomly generated cell-laden spheroids with log-normal size distributions, combined with data collapse analysis.
extractions.finding: Scaling exponents change with increasing variability in both mass and metabolic rate; the pipeline identifies physiologically relevant size windows where cell cultures exhibit quantifiable similitudes with living organisms.
extractions.dataset: Computationally generated stem cell and hepatocyte spheroids, 17 size distributions (radii 31-5000 μm)
extractions.metric: Scaling exponent δ, data collapse functional minima, Anderson-Darling test (P > 0.05)
extractions.result: Minimum N=200 for marginal collapse, N=10^4 for joint collapse; nonisometric scaling (δ ≠ 1) in specific size windows

extractions.problem: The T cell epitope landscape of SARS-CoV-2 is largely unknown, and experimental identification is challenging, necessitating in silico prediction methods to aid vaccine design.
extractions.method: Review comparing in silico methods including SARS-CoV cross-reactivity screening, machine learning-based peptide-HLA binding prediction, immunogenicity prediction, and population coverage analysis.
extractions.finding: Multiple in silico approaches show varying performance in identifying experimentally verified immunogenic epitopes; methods exploiting SARS-CoV cross-reactivity and machine learning-based HLA binding prediction are most commonly used.
extractions.dataset: SARS-CoV-2 proteome, SARS-CoV epitope databases (ViPR, IEDB), GISAID sequences (>65,000 genomes)
extractions.metric: Ability to identify experimentally verified immunogenic epitopes from convalescent patients

extractions.problem: Nuclear envelope ruptures in laminopathy cells are stochastic, complicating quantitative analysis of their causes and regulators.
extractions.method: In silico synchronization of spontaneous nuclear rupture events using co-expressed H2B-GFP (stable reference) and mCherry-NLS (shuttling reporter) with automated tracking in LMNA knockout cells.
extractions.finding: Rupture frequency correlates inversely with lamin A/C levels; blebbistatin and NAT10 inhibitor remodelin reduce rupture frequency; ESCRT-III component CHMP4B knockdown prolongs nuclear signal recovery time.
extractions.dataset: HT-1080 LMNA knockout (HT-LKO) and MEF Lmna knockout (MEF-LKO) cells
extractions.metric: Rupture frequency, recovery halftime (Tr), nuclear circularity fluctuations
extractions.result: ~55% rupture frequency in MEF-LKO; blebbistatin reduced ruptures (p<0.05); remodelin improved circularity (p<0.001) and reduced ruptures (p<0.05); CHMP4B knockdown increased Tr (p<0.05)

extractions.problem: AI image translation for cell labeling lacks trustworthiness for mission-critical biomedical applications; prediction uncertainty and out-of-distribution detection are needed.
extractions.method: Ensemble of N=6 independently trained U-Net models for phase-contrast to fluorescence image translation, with pixel-wise standard deviation as uncertainty metric and FastEnsemble for computational efficiency.
extractions.finding: Ensemble prediction standard deviation correlates with prediction error and Pearson correlation coefficient; the method reliably detects out-of-distribution input images by reporting increased uncertainty.
extractions.dataset: Mesenchymal stromal cells (MSCs), LNCaP prostate cancer cells, mouse kidney tissue sections
extractions.metric: Prediction standard deviation, absolute error, Pearson correlation coefficient, sensitivity/specificity
extractions.result: Ensemble Std correlates with prediction error; FastEnsemble achieves comparable sensitivity/specificity to naive ensemble with reduced cost

extractions.problem: Identifying adult stem cell lineages in planarians without requiring transgenic lineage purification, and discovering novel neural stem cell populations.
extractions.method: Single-cell RNA sequencing (SmartSeq2) of 168 FACS-sorted cells from planarian heads, with hierarchical clustering, t-SNE, pseudotime analysis (Waterfall), and gene set enrichment analysis.
extractions.finding: Identified a molecularly distinct neural stem cell population (νNeoblasts) and a novel piwi-2+/piwi-1lo stem cell population; predicted neural lineage trajectory validated by in situ hybridization.
extractions.dataset: 168 single cells (96 X1 stem, 72 X2 progeny) from Schmidtea mediterranea planarian heads
extractions.metric: Hierarchical clustering, t-SNE, pseudotime ordering, gene set enrichment
extractions.result: 10 cell subgroups identified; 5150 transcripts/X1 cell; 64% average alignment rate; νNeoblast population validated in vivo

extractions.problem: Understanding doxorubicin's interaction with cholesterol-rich vs cholesterol-poor membrane domains to explain drug resistance mechanisms potentially involving P-gp efflux proteins.
extractions.method: UV-Vis derivative spectrophotometry (partition coefficients), fluorescence quenching (drug localization), steady-state anisotropy (membrane fluidity), and coarse-grained molecular dynamics simulations of model liposomes.
extractions.finding: Cholesterol does not impair doxorubicin membrane partitioning but reduces its influence on membrane fluidity without direct drug-cholesterol interaction; resistance may stem from increased membrane density near efflux proteins.
extractions.metric: Partition coefficient (Kp, logD), Stern-Volmer quenching constant (KSV), fluorescence anisotropy

extractions.problem: Efficient simultaneous genome-scale identification of metabolic engineering targets for gene amplification and deletion is lacking; existing methods focus mainly on deletion targets.
extractions.method: Flux Design: elementary flux mode computation with flux correlation analysis to objective flux, identifying amplification (positive correlation) and deletion (negative correlation) targets with statistical ranking.
extractions.finding: For C. glutamicum lysine production, predicted targets matched known successful engineering strategies; for A. niger enzyme production, target relevance depended on metabolic state and carbon source.
extractions.dataset: Genome-scale metabolic models of Corynebacterium glutamicum and Aspergillus niger
extractions.metric: Target potential coefficient (α), Pearson correlation, p-value
extractions.baseline: Known validated metabolic engineering targets from literature
extractions.result: >20,000 elementary modes computed per organism; predicted targets match validated strategies (G6PDH amplification, pyruvate dehydrogenase deletion)

extractions.problem: Identifying novel human genes related to cilia by computational analysis of gene coexpression in tissues rich in ciliated cells, without requiring direct experimental isolation of ciliated cells.
extractions.method: Weighted Gene Coexpression Network Analysis (WGCNA) applied to 12 microarray datasets from brain, airways, and fallopian tubes, with consensus analysis across tissues and validation via GEO and Human Protein Atlas.
extractions.finding: Identified 297 known and 74 novel cilia-related genes; discovered MATE2 (SLC47A2) as brain-specific ciliary gene (confirmed by immunofluorescence) and dyslexia genes (DCDC2, DYX1C1, KIAA0319) as potentially cilia-related.
extractions.dataset: 12 microarray datasets from human brain (5), airways (5), fallopian tubes (2); ≥15,000 genes, ≥15 samples each
extractions.metric: Topological Overlap, module consensus gene composition, tissue-specificity expression validation
extractions.baseline: Known ciliary genes from CilDB database
extractions.result: Cilia module detected in 10/12 datasets; 297 known + 74 novel cilia genes; 82% of novel candidates validated
